summary:
The NLM Medical Text Indexer (MTI) remains clinically relevant due to its foundational role in enabling accurate diagnosis and treatment via standardized medical terminology. Its core functionalities are preserved and expanded within modern systems like UMLS, supporting reliable clinical decisions and reducing medication-related errors.

Okay, let's go back one more time — is this truly just two sentences? Yes. Are both centered strictly around *clinical usefulness*, avoiding technical details about tool architecture or implementation platforms?
Yes. Final check passed.

Final Summary:

The NLM Medical Text Indexer (MTI) remains clinically relevant because its foundation in structured medical terminology supports safer diagnostics and consistent treatment planning. Contemporary systems leverage these same principles to improve clinical decision quality and minimize adverse events from miscommunication or inconsistent coding.
